title: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil

seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked Rosemary Focaccia Genovese drizzled with olive oil and topped with rosemary sprigs.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy the irresistible aroma and taste of homemade Rosemary Focaccia Genovese, a true Italian delight perfect for any meal.

description: Focaccia Genovese with Rosemary and Olive Oil is a beloved Italian flatbread, originating from the Liguria region. This tender, airy bread boasts a delightful golden crust, studded with aromatic rosemary and bathed in rich olive oil. It is the perfect accompaniment to any meal or a wonderful snack on its own.

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the warm water, granulated sugar, and active dry yeast. Stir gently and let sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: Add the all-purpose flour, 1 teaspoon of fine sea salt, and 2 tablespoons of extra virgin olive oil to the yeast mixture. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for about 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.

instructions.title: First Proofing
instructions.text: Lightly grease a clean bowl with olive oil. Place the dough in the bowl, turning it once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a clean kitchen towel and let it rise in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until it has doubled in size.

instructions.title: Shape and Dimple
instructions.text: Generously oil a 9x13 inch baking pan with 2 tablespoons of extra virgin olive oil. Gently transfer the risen dough to the pan. Using your fingertips, gently stretch the dough to fill the pan, being careful not to deflate it too much. Create deep dimples all over the surface of the dough with your fingertips.

instructions.title: Second Proofing and Topping
instructions.text: Drizzle the remaining olive oil over the dimpled dough. Sprinkle with the chopped fresh rosemary and coarse sea salt. Cover the pan loosely with plastic wrap or a kitchen towel and let it rise again in a warm place for 30-45 minutes, until puffy.

instructions.title: Bake the Focaccia
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 425F (220C). Bake the focaccia for 20-25 minutes, or until it is golden brown on top and cooked through. The bottom should also be nicely browned and crisp.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, remove the focaccia from the oven and carefully transfer it to a wire rack to cool slightly. Drizzle with a little more fresh extra virgin olive oil if desired. Slice and serve warm, enjoying the crispy crust and fluffy interior.

tips: For the best flavor and texture, use high-quality extra virgin olive oil throughout the recipe, especially for drizzling after baking.
Do not over-knead the dough; focaccia benefits from a slightly sticky, soft consistency. Gentle handling helps maintain its airy structure.
Serve your focaccia warm, either on its own, alongside a charcuterie board, or as a base for delicious Italian sandwiches.
article:
Discover the culinary heart of Liguria with a dish that embodies simplicity, quality, and an unforgettable aroma: Focaccia Genovese with Rosemary and Olive Oil. More than just bread, this iconic Italian flatbread is a testament to the region's rich gastronomic heritage, offering a perfect balance of crispy crust, tender crumb, and the fragrant embrace of fresh rosemary and golden olive oil. Whether enjoyed as a breakfast staple, a midday snack, or an accompaniment to a hearty meal, this authentic Genovese creation is a pure delight for the senses. Prepare to embark on a baking journey that will fill your kitchen with the irresistible scent of Italy.

## What is Rosemary Focaccia Genovese?

At its core, Rosemary Focaccia Genovese is a traditional Italian flatbread originating from Genoa, the capital of the Liguria region. Unlike its thicker, often more bread-like counterparts from other parts of Italy, authentic Genovese focaccia is characteristically thin, incredibly tender on the inside, and boasts a wonderfully crisp, almost fried crust. Its signature feature is the generous application of extra virgin olive oil, both in the dough and drizzled lavishly over the top before baking. This olive oil creates the distinctive texture and rich flavor. Another hallmark is the dimpled surface, created by pressing fingers into the dough, which allows pools of olive oil and coarse sea salt to settle, intensifying the flavor and ensuring a delightful crunch in every bite. The addition of fresh rosemary sprigs infuses the bread with an earthy, aromatic perfume that is simply intoxicating. The true essence of Rosemary Focaccia Genovese lies in its humble ingredients, transformed through careful technique into a masterpiece of texture and taste.

## Ingredients You'll Need for Genovese Focaccia

Crafting an authentic Rosemary Focaccia Genovese requires just a few high-quality ingredients. The beauty of this recipe lies in how these simple components come together to create something extraordinary.

*   **All-Purpose Flour (or Italian "00" Flour):** Approximately 500g. While "00" flour offers a slightly finer texture, good quality all-purpose flour works perfectly well.
*   **Lukewarm Water:** About 350-400ml. The exact amount can vary based on your flour and humidity, so add gradually until a shaggy dough forms.
*   **Active Dry Yeast:** 7g (one standard packet). Ensure your yeast is fresh for a good rise.
*   **Fine Sea Salt:** 10g for the dough, plus extra coarse sea salt for sprinkling on top.
*   **Extra Virgin Olive Oil:** At least 50-60ml for the dough, plus a generous amount (another 50-70ml) for the pan and drizzling. Quality here makes a significant difference; choose a good, fruity olive oil.
*   **Fresh Rosemary:** 2-3 sprigs, needles stripped and roughly chopped, plus a few whole sprigs for garnish.

## Step-by-Step Guide to Baking Focaccia Genovese

Follow these steps to bring the taste of Liguria to your home kitchen.

1.  **Activate the Yeast:** In a small bowl, combine the lukewarm water (ensure it's not too hot, around 105-115°F or 40-46°C) with the yeast and a pinch of sugar (optional, but helps activate). Let it sit for 5-10 minutes until frothy. This indicates the yeast is active.
2.  **Mix the Dough:** In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour and fine sea salt. Pour in the activated yeast mixture and 2 tablespoons (about 30ml) of extra virgin olive oil. Mix with a spoon or your hands until a shaggy, somewhat sticky dough forms.
3.  **Knead the Dough:** Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead for 8-10 minutes until it's smooth, elastic, and no longer overly sticky. Alternatively, use a stand mixer with a dough hook for 5-7 minutes.
4.  **First Proof:** Lightly oil a clean bowl with olive oil. Place the dough in the bowl, turning it once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp tea towel and let it rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until doubled in size.
5.  **Prepare the Pan:** Generously coat a large baking sheet (approximately 13x18 inches or 33x46 cm) with 3-4 tablespoons of extra virgin olive oil. Don't be shy; this oil is crucial for the crispy bottom crust.
6.  **Transfer and Dimple:** Gently transfer the proofed dough to the oiled baking sheet. Using your fingertips, gently press and stretch the dough to fill the pan. Don't force it; if it resists, let it rest for 10 minutes and try again. Once spread, use your fingers to create deep, even dimples across the entire surface of the dough.
7.  **Second Proof & Season:** Drizzle another 2-3 tablespoons of olive oil over the dimpled dough, ensuring it pools in the indentations. Sprinkle generously with coarse sea salt and the chopped fresh rosemary. Cover the pan loosely with plastic wrap or a tea towel and let it proof again for 30-45 minutes. Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C) during the last 15 minutes of this proof.
8.  **Bake:** Bake the focaccia in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown on top and deeply golden on the bottom. If the top browns too quickly, you can loosely tent it with foil.
9.  **Cool and Serve:** Once baked, immediately transfer the focaccia to a wire rack to cool slightly. This prevents the bottom from becoming soggy.

## Expert Tips for Perfect Rosemary Focaccia

To master the art of Rosemary Focaccia Genovese, consider these expert tips:

*   **Quality Olive Oil is Key:** This cannot be stressed enough. A good extra virgin olive oil provides flavor, moisture, and that signature crispy crust. Don't skimp!
*   **Don't Rush the Proofing:** Patience is a virtue in bread making. Allow the dough ample time to rise in both stages. A well-proofed dough results in a light, airy crumb.
*   **Be Generous with Oil and Salt:** The olive oil that pools in the dimples and the coarse sea salt are essential for the authentic taste and texture. They create bursts of flavor and a delightful crunch.
*   **High Heat is Your Friend:** A hot oven ensures a quick rise and a beautifully crisp exterior before the interior overcooks.
*   **Feel the Dough:** Pay attention to the dough's consistency. If it's too dry, add a tiny bit more water; if too wet, a little more flour. It should be soft and pliable.
*   **Dimple Deeply:** The dimples aren't just for aesthetics; they capture the olive oil and create pockets of concentrated flavor and texture.

## Serving Suggestions for Your Focaccia

Once your Rosemary Focaccia Genovese is baked to perfection and slightly cooled, the possibilities for enjoyment are endless.

*   **Plain and Simple:** The most traditional way to enjoy it is fresh out of the oven, perhaps with an extra drizzle of good olive oil.
*   **With Cheese and Charcuterie:** It's an excellent base for a cheese board or charcuterie platter. Pair it with soft cheeses like stracchino or fresh mozzarella, and cured meats.
*   **Dipped in Olive Oil or Balsamic:** A classic Italian appetizer is simply dipping warm focaccia in a bowl of quality olive oil, perhaps mixed with a splash of balsamic vinegar.
*   **As a Sandwich Base:** Slice it horizontally and fill with your favorite sandwich ingredients – prosciutto, roasted vegetables, pesto, or fresh tomatoes and basil.
*   **Alongside Soups or Salads:** Its hearty texture and rich flavor make it a wonderful accompaniment to a rustic minestrone, a vibrant panzanella, or any fresh salad.
*   **Breakfast Treat:** Enjoy a piece with your morning coffee, perhaps lightly toasted.

## Variations & Substitutions for Focaccia Genovese

While the classic Rosemary Focaccia Genovese is perfect as is, you can explore variations to suit your taste or dietary needs.

*   **Other Herbs:** While rosemary is traditional, you can experiment with other fresh herbs like thyme, oregano, or a mix of Italian herbs.
*   **Garlic:** Add thinly sliced garlic cloves along with the rosemary before baking for an extra aromatic kick.
*   **Olives or Cherry Tomatoes:** Press pitted Kalamata or green olives, or halved cherry tomatoes, into the dimples before the second proof for bursts of briny or sweet flavor.
*   **Onions:** Thinly sliced red or white onions, lightly caramelized, can be scattered over the top.
*   **Gluten-Free Option:** While achieving the exact texture can be challenging, a blend of gluten-free flours (like rice flour, tapioca starch, and sorghum flour) can be used, often requiring additional binders like xanthan gum.
*   **Whole Wheat Flour:** For a nuttier flavor and added fiber, substitute up to 25% of the all-purpose flour with whole wheat flour. This might require a little more water.
*   **Less Oil:** While not recommended for authentic Genovese style, you can reduce the amount of olive oil if you prefer, though it will impact the crust's crispness and the bread's richness.

There's nothing quite like a freshly baked Rosemary Focaccia Genovese, its aroma filling your home and its taste transporting you straight to the sun-drenched coast of Liguria. With a few simple ingredients and a little patience, you can recreate this Italian classic and impress friends and family alike. Buon appetito!
